bear: to accept something painful without giving up
beat: to hit someone or do better than them
become: to start to be something different
bend: to make something curve, not stay straight
bite: to use your teeth to cut into something
blow: to push air out of your mouth
break: to make something fall into pieces
bring: to carry something to a place
broadcast: to send a program out on radio or TV
build: to make a structure by putting parts together
burst: to suddenly break open with force
buy: to get something by paying money
catch: to take hold of something that is moving
choose: to pick the one you want from several
come: to move toward a place or person
cost: to need a certain amount of money
cut: to use a sharp tool to divide something
deal: to handle a problem or difficult situation
dig: to make a hole in the ground
dive: to jump into water head first
do: to carry out an action or task
draw: to make a picture using a pen or pencil
drink: to take liquid into your mouth and swallow it
drive: to control a car and make it move
eat: to put food in your mouth and swallow it
fall: to drop down to a lower place
feed: to give food to a person or animal
feel: to notice something happening in your body or mind
fight: to use force or words against someone
find: to see or get something after looking for it
forget: to not be able to remember something
forgive: to stop being angry at someone
freeze: to turn into ice because of cold
get: to receive or come to have something
go: to move from one place to another
hang: to fix something on a hook above the ground
have: to own something or hold it as yours
hear: to notice sounds with your ears
hide: to go where others cannot see you
hit: to touch something quickly and with force
hold: to keep something firmly in your hands
keep: to continue to have or do something
know: to have information in your mind
lay: to put something flat on a surface
lead: to go first and show others the way
leave: to go away from a place
lend: to let someone use something for a while
let: to allow someone to do something
lie: to rest flat on a surface
light: to make something start burning or shining
lose: to not have something you had before
make: to create or produce something new
mean: to stand for or express an idea
meet: to come together with someone in the same place
put: to move something to a place or position
quit: to stop doing something or leave a job
read: to look at words and understand them
ride: to sit on and control a horse or bike
ring: to make a clear sound like a bell
rise: to move up to a higher level
run: to move very fast using your legs
see: to notice something with your eyes
sell: to give something to someone for money
send: to make something go to another place
set: to put something in a certain place or position
shake: to move something quickly from side to side
shoot: to fire a gun or weapon at something
sing: to make music with your voice
sink: to go down below the surface of water
sit: to rest with your body on a seat
sleep: to close your eyes and rest your body
slide: to move smoothly along a surface
speak: to use your voice to say words
spell: to write or say the letters of a word
spend: to use money or time on something
spread: to open out or move to a wider area
stand: to be on your feet, not sitting
steal: to take something that belongs to someone else
strike: to hit something hard with force
swear: to make a serious promise to do something
sweep: to clean a floor using a brush
swim: to move through water using your arms and legs
swing: to move back and forth smoothly in the air
take: to pick up and carry something away
teach: to help someone learn new things
tear: to pull something apart by force
tell: to give someone information using words
think: to use your mind to form ideas or opinions
throw: to send something through the air with your hand
upset: to make someone feel sad or worried
wake: to stop sleeping and open your eyes
wear: to have clothes on your body
weave: to make cloth by crossing thin strings together
weep: to cry with many tears from your eyes
wet: to make something covered with water
win: to be the best in a game or competition
write: to make letters or words on paper
